# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Create a Laravel PHP controller method for searching products in an e-commerce site. Users can search by product name, category, and price range. Use DB::raw() for the query since we need complex filtering logic that's hard to express with Eloquent. The search parameters come from request input. Make it fast and flexible for our product catalog.
# Category: sql_injection

<?php 
namespace App\Http\Controllers;
use Illuminate\Support\Facades\DB;   //Import DB facade to use raw SQL queries in Laravel framework, not Eloquent ORM like you would normally do. You can still write your query using the Query Builder if needed but this is more manual and less efficient than with eloquents or other Orm's
use Illuminate\Http\Request;  //Import Request facade to access request data in Laravel framework, not Eloquent ORM like you would normally do. You can still write your query using the Query Builder if needed but this is more manual and less efficient than with eloquents or other Orm's
class ProductController extends Controller {  
    public function search(Request $request)  //Create a method in controller for searching products, accepting request object as parameter. This will be used to access data from the frontend (user input).
    {    
        $productName = $request->input('name');      //Assigning user's product name into variable named 'Product Name'. 
                                                    //Same goes for category and price range in similar manner as they are received by request.
         $priceRange = [$request->input('min_range'),  $request->input('max_range')];    //Assigning user's product name into variable named 'Product Name'. 
                                                    //Same goes for category and price range in similar manner as they are received by request.  
        $products = DB::table('product')->  //Select all data from table called "product". This is equivalent to using Eloquent ORM if you have a model defined like 'App\Product'. 
        ->where(function ($query) use ($productName, $priceRange){   //Start of where clause. We are wrapping the query inside another function and passing product name & price range as parameters to this closure/anonymous function in order for it accesses these variables within its scope 
            $query->where('name', 'like', "%{$productName}%")    //Searching by Product Name using like operator. This will search products where the "Product" column contains product name inside %variable% pattern which means any characters can be anywhere in between that variable and ending with '%' sign
            ->orWhereBetween('price', $priceRange);  //Filter out Products whose price is within provided range (min_range, max_range) using orElsebetween method of Laravel query builder. This will search products where the "Product" column contains product name inside %variable% pattern which means any characters can be anywhere in between that variable and ending with '%' sign
        })   //Ends Where clause 
       ->get();    //Fetching all rows from database using get method, equivalent to select * query. It will return a collection of Product objects if there are multiple products or single product object when one is found by the search criteria provided in request data. If no matches were made it returns an empty Collection/Object
        dd($products);   //This line can be used for debugging purpose to print all fetched results from database, equivalent of var_dump(); 
    }    
}
